pi_items.source: Docker documentation confirms that when bip is set to 192.168.200.1/24, the bridge subnet derives to 192.168.200.0/24. See https://docs.docker.com/engine/network/drivers/bridge/ and https://docs.docker.com/reference/cli/docker/network/create/

Docker documentation confirms that the gateway IP equals the bridge IP from the bip setting. When bip is set to 192.168.200.1/24, the gateway is 192.168.200.1. Multiple sources confirm this pattern: https://docs.docker.com/engine/network/drivers/bridge/, https://mrkaran.dev/tils/docker-custom-subnet/, https://tryfix.it.com/what-is-the-default-ip-range-of-docker-guide-to-bridge-networking/

Docker daemon configuration documentation confirms 'bip' is the parameter name for bridge IP. Sources: https://docs.docker.com/engine/network/drivers/bridge/, https://docs.docker.com/reference/cli/dockerd/, and GitHub issues discussing bip configuration: https://github.com/docker/for-mac/issues/7104, https://forums.docker.com/t/how-can-i-pass-the-bip-argument-change-bridge-network-ip/15346

privileged_info_summary: The task specifies three core Docker bridge configuration parameters, all verified against authoritative Docker documentation: (1) The bridge subnet must be 192.168.200.0/24, which is derived when the daemon bip parameter is set to 192.168.200.1/24; (2) The gateway IP must be 192.168.200.1, which is the bridge's own IP address on the configured subnet; (3) The configuration key is 'bip' (Bridge IP), which is the standard Docker daemon parameter for configuring the default bridge network subnet. The CIDR notation format (192.168.200.1/24) is required and must end with .1. Docker's docker network inspect command reports these values via IPAM.Config.Subnet and IPAM.Config.Gateway fields.
